participant_local_context: lio_vale is present for the visible event or receives it through local supervisor/worker channels.
The participant has access only to observed action, speech, role knowledge, current scene state, and established local relationships.
observable_event_summary: Ilya steps to the operations glass comm panel, raises one hand toward the Kappa-7 threshold, and tags the access route hold on the supervisor board. Kappa-7 is on procedural hold. No one enters the artery until seal, air, and thermal are reconciled against the board and a baseline anchor check is logged. Vale, take the clearance freeze. Dax, put an anchor pair on verification, not entry. Nara-7, keep your team staged outside the threshold and state the readout sequence for the log.
known_facts: Kappa-7 is a dangerous service artery with live seal-air-thermal consequences.
The event is visible enough to change shop-floor expectations.
Actor intent is not directly known.
active_pressures: Preserve or challenge authority without losing the room.
Decide whether the incident means safety, malfunction, labor action, or liability.
perceived_relationships: Existing Pallas agent-state relationship stances apply, but this appraisal records only the participant local read after this event.
interpretation: Lio sees a useful safety opening and a record-language trap in the same move.
confidence_notes: Manual pilot appraisal. Confidence is qualitative because the appraiser is currently coordinator-reviewed rather than model-produced.
